Assembly: The assembly icDruCana1.hap1.1 is based on 154x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Haplotypic inversions were detected on Chromosome X from 3.86 to the end and on Chromosome 8 from 3.2 to 11.5 Mbp. The region on Chromosome 9 from 10.9 to the end is repetitive and the order and orientation may be uncertain. The X chromosome was identified based on BUSCO ancestral hits. Some centromeric regions look to be missing, they are not present in the assembly.

Assembly: The assembly ilUdeLute1.hap2.1 is based on 54x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 1 (6.6 - 8.4 Mbp) are unknown. Z chromosome identified based BUSCO gene painting with ancestral Merian elements (https://doi.org/10.1038/s41559-024-02329-4).

Assembly: The assembly ilLycTher1.hap1.1 is based on 55x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This genome has been assembled using PacBio and HiC data and phased. The result is two curated haplotypes. Z chromosome identified based BUSCO gene painting with ancestral Merian elements (https://doi.org/10.1038/s41559-024-02329-4).

Assembly: The assembly jsCasSpea1.1 is based on 201x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Aquatic Symbiosis Genomics Project (https://www.aquaticsymbiosisgenomics.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Order and orientation of scaffolds from ~10.5-11.31Mb on Chromosome 1, ~10.4-10.94Mb on Chromosome 2, ~7.84-8.64Mb on Chromosome 4, ~6.83-7.77Mb on Chromosome 6, and ~8.32-9.46Mb on Chromosome 8 is unsure.

Assembly: The assembly idPhyRufi1.1 is based on 46x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. All chromosomes are named in size order. We did not identify the sex chromosome(s) as sequence data from the heterogametic sex was not available and homology is unreliable for sex chromosome identification in Diptera due to frequent sex chromosome turnover (https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pbio.1002078).
